session.name: MPI-215 Model Library overlay — brainstorm + design + plan (design frozen, ready to build)

goal.original: Redesign the Models page: move it from the narrow slide-over into a full-page overlay with a grid of model cards, and add Image/Video filters. Soon there will be hundreds of models.
goal.summary: Design fully explored, approved, and frozen as a working reference mockup. Compact plan is written and attached to MPI-215 (todo, planned). No code written yet. Fresh session runs mpi-continue to build end-to-end and matches the mockup.

completed: Design brainstormed and approved (Option 1: Models opens overlay directly; slide-over keeps settings/hotkeys/queue).
Layout chosen: Contact Sheet (variant B) — preview-led tiles.
Full interaction model resolved: lean uniform tiles + click-to-open right-drawer detail panel.
Working reference mockup built + verified (search, media/size filters, split-block grid, video hover-play, detail panel all functional).
Compact plan written and attached; card maturity idea->planned.
pending: Build the Model Library overlay end to end (single Implementation item in plan.md), matching the reference mockup.
Stop for user UX verification of the running overlay (verify mode: user-ux).

completed: Plan.md carries full design spec, reference-mockup path, architecture decisions, and a Preservation Notes section listing which .claude/rules to offer updating on completion.
pending: On completion: offer to update .claude/rules/component-mounts.md (MpiModelManager now overlay-hosted not slide-over; new detail drawer; fix the ~line 43 note calling it a slide-over content component), component-events.md (models:open -> overlay), component-state.md if new state keys added.
On completion: add an UNRELEASED changelog entry for the Model Library.
Doc-drift rule (CLAUDE.md): ask user before editing architectural rule files.

next_action.description: Read the reference mockup (open model-library-B.html — it is the authoritative visual + interaction spec), read plan.md, then build the Model Library overlay end to end: (1) route models:open to open MpiModelManager inside MpiOverlay body-mode instead of slide-over; (2) restyle into 'Model Library' with dark ground + Media/Size/search filters; (3) split-block grid (Installed/Available, each Image 4:5 then Video 16:9 sub-grid); (4) lean uniform tiles with inline install-state + progress bar + absolute recently-installed heat dot; (5) right-drawer detail panel INSIDE the overlay DOM reusing MpiSlideOver visual chrome (NOT its singleton) carrying moved card logic (op toggles, arch toggles, VRAM table, description, install/update/uninstall). Then stop for user UX verification against the mockup.

key_decisions: Models opens the full-page overlay DIRECTLY (Option 1). The app slide-over is NOT used for models anymore; it stays reserved for settings/hotkeys/queue, unchanged.

Layout = Contact Sheet: preview-led tiles, NOT an identical card grid (DESIGN.md + impeccable both ban identical grids). The mixed aspect ratios (image 4:5, video 16:9) plus split Image/Video sub-blocks provide visual rhythm without an artificial 'featured' tile.
Ragged-hole fix: within each section render an Image sub-grid then a Video sub-grid — each holds ONE aspect ratio so rows align.

Card payload (description, op toggles, arch toggles, VRAM table, disk, install/uninstall) does NOT fit a lean preview tile AND video-hover=play conflicts with hover-to-reveal. Resolution: lean tile + CLICK opens a right-drawer detail panel holding all controls.

CRITICAL stacking decision: MpiOverlay body-mode renders ABOVE MpiSlideOver (user confirmed). So the detail panel must be a drawer rendered INSIDE the overlay's own DOM (position:absolute child), reusing MpiSlideOver's visual CHROME/CSS only — NOT the app-level slide-over:open singleton (which would sit behind the overlay).
Image/Video filter reads existing model.mediaType field directly — NO data-model change, no op-prefix parsing (earlier brainstorm idea superseded).
Reuse existing MpiModelManager install/op/arch/VRAM/download logic (MPI-122/163/168/200/209) — MOVE it into the detail panel, do NOT reimplement (would regress hard-won correctness).
Recently-installed marker = heat dot ABSOLUTE on the thumb (zero layout shift). An earlier text-line marker was rejected because it changed card height and broke grid alignment.

Design tooling: the 'impeccable' skill (pbakaus/impeccable, installed globally this session) is the design authority the user wants — not the local frontend-design skill. It is model-invocable via the Skill tool now.
constraints: CONCURRENT AGENT is editing model-installation logic inside MpiModelManager.js. Edit by content anchors (never line numbers), re-read on stale-Edit errors, re-grep after each edit, reconcile if both touched the same region. Commit by explicit pathspec, never git add -A.

This is a NEW surface: consulted docs/redesign/PRODUCT.md + DESIGN.md during design. Stage design system authoritative: mono JetBrains Mono, sharp radius (--r-1:0), heat accent theatrical-only, no hardcoded colors (use tokens from styles/01_base.css), BEM class naming, ComponentFactory.create, register CSS in preloadStyles.js, document props in types.js.

Dark surface tokens (final target from the mockup): --lib-bg oklch(0.26 0.020 350), --lib-card oklch(0.33 0.022 350), hover ~0.37. The user has a darkness tuner in the mockup; if unsure of exact value, confirm.

verify mode is user-ux: after mechanical self-verification (routing, filters, no console errors, install/download fire, tests green), STOP for the user to look at the running overlay and confirm it matches the approved mockup before marking done.
Launch the real Electron app to verify (unset ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE, npm start bg, poll /comfy/get-path). Screenshot the overlay + an open detail panel and compare against model-library-B.html.

resume_prompt: You are resuming work on Cubric Vision task MPI-215 "Model Library overlay" (branch RunPod). Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/7737ca2b-517a-483c-aaf9-b542d483a1eb.json and .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-215/plan.md, then run mpi-continue to build. The design is FULLY APPROVED and frozen as a working reference mockup at C:\Users\Fabio\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\c--AI-Mpi-Cubric-Vision\d6c3f9a0-cb79-4e4b-beb8-4f38e1bd7ff8\scratchpad\model-library-B.html — OPEN IT FIRST; it is the authoritative visual + interaction spec, and the built feature must look and behave like it. Build end to end: (1) change js/shell.js models:open (~line 344) to open MpiModelManager inside MpiOverlay body-mode instead of emitting slide-over:open; (2) restyle MpiModelManager into 'Model Library' — dark ground oklch(0.26 0.020 350)/cards oklch(0.33 0.022 350), Media (Image/Video via model.mediaType) + Size (Low/Balanced/High) + live search filters; (3) split-block grid: per section Installed then Available, each an Image sub-grid (4:5) then a Video sub-grid (16:9) so rows align; (4) lean uniform tiles — preview (image still, video plays on hover, no play button), name, category·tier, media badge, fixed-height inline state (Installed/Install/live progress bar), recently-installed heat dot absolute on the thumb; (5) a right-drawer detail panel rendered INSIDE the overlay's own DOM (NOT the app slide-over singleton — overlays stack ABOVE the slide-over, so a real slide-over would be hidden behind) reusing MpiSlideOver's visual chrome, opened on tile click, carrying the EXISTING MpiModelManager card logic MOVED here: description, Operations toggles (MPI-122), GPU-weight arch toggles (MPI-200/209, video/arch models only), VRAM->RAM table (MPI-168, video only), disk size, Install/Update/Uninstall. CRITICAL: a concurrent agent is editing model-installation logic in MpiModelManager.js — edit by content anchors, re-read on stale errors, re-grep after each edit. Reuse existing install/download logic; do not reimplement it. Follow Stage design tokens (styles/01_base.css), BEM, ComponentFactory, register CSS in preloadStyles.js, document props in types.js — no hardcoded colors. Verify mode is user-ux: after self-verifying routing/filters/no-console-errors in the real Electron app and screenshotting the overlay, STOP for the user to confirm it matches the mockup before marking done.
